title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
In a garden, Sedrick discusses a family member's questionable wartime service and suspicious food purchases with Lucy, an expert in domestic service. Emma interrupts the conversation, appearing troubled. She expresses concern to Sedrick that the murder victim might be Martina, a French woman her brother was supposed to marry before he died in the war. Emma notes that Martina sent a telegram claiming she had to return to France just as she was scheduled to visit. Sedrick dismisses the connection as nonsense and advises Emma to remain silent and let the police confirm the corpse's identity, suggesting that Harold and Alfred would agree.

Emma later encounters Dr. Campbell near the rear driveway. She confides in him her anxiety regarding the possibility that the deceased is Martina. Dr. Campbell, acting as a supportive friend, encourages Emma to follow her own judgment and promises his support, suggesting that staying silent might only prolong her anxiety.

Meanwhile, Mr. Crickenshaw calls Lucy into his dusty, unused study. He shows her his collection of geological specimens and then points to a large, faded family tree on the wall. He claims descent from royalty through his mother's side, citing ancestors such as Edward the Confessor and King Eadred. He expresses resentment toward his late father, whom he describes as a vulgar merchant, and asserts that he inherited his mother's superior qualities and taste.
